title: playerTickBeams reads MP other-player weaponnum via raw obj+0x80 (hits maxdamage) — wrong walk anim

evidence.path: src/game/bondview.c:25111/25119/25127/25139 call bondwalkItemCheckBitflags(*(s8*)((u8*)rightObj+0x80),...) on ObjectRecord* (WeaponObjRecord.weaponnum, N64 0x80). ObjectRecord native 0x90 -> weaponnum@0x90; raw 0x80 reads maxdamage low byte -> wrong walk-anim type. Inside the FID-0094 fn but NOT covered by the FID-0094 gate; ungated. MP other-player walk-anim (cosmetic). Fix: ((WeaponObjRecord*)rightObj)->weaponnum.

src/game/bondview.c:25124 playerTickBeams reads the MP other-player weapon flag via raw *(s8*)((u8*)rightObj+0x80); ObjectRecord grew +0x10 on the 64-bit port so 0x80 no longer lands on WeaponObjRecord.weaponnum (now native 0x90) — it reads into maxdamage, yielding the wrong walk/rifle animation for other players.

src/game/bondview.c:24745 added portNoMpWalkAnimWeaponnumFix() accessor (GE007_NO_MP_WALK_ANIM_WEAPONNUM_FIX) + _Static_assert(offsetof(WeaponObjRecord,weaponnum)==0x90); gating the 4 reads to read the named field by default.

src/game/bondview.c:25124 4 reads now ((WeaponObjRecord*)obj)->weaponnum by default, raw offset restorable via GE007_NO_MP_WALK_ANIM_WEAPONNUM_FIX=1. Build clean, _Static_assert holds, tape_regression.sh byte-identical on all 7 SP tapes (MP path not exercised).
